content.problemStatement:
Given a binary string SSS of length NNN, check if you can transform it to another binary string TTT (also of length NNN) by using **either** of the following operations as many times as needed :


Pick (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and swap SiS_iSi​ and SjS_jSj​
Pick (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and flip both SiS_iSi​ and SjS_jSj​. Here, to flip xxx it means to convert it to 1−x1 - x1−x.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the lengths of the string SSS and TTT
The second line contains SSS - a binary string of size NNN
The third line contains TTT - a binary string of size NNN




Output Format
For each test case, output YES\text{YES}YES if it is possible to convert SSS to TTT using the operation several times, otherwise output NO\text{NO}NO


It is allowed to output each character in either case. yes\text{yes}yes, YeS\text{YeS}YeS will also be considered as a positive response.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 and flip them. Then, SSS becomes 111111 equal to TTT
Test Case 4 : We perform the following sequence of operations :

Choose i=3,j=4i = 3, j = 4i=3,j=4 and swap them. Then SSS becomes 000100010001
Choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 and flip them. Then, SSS becomes 110111011101

Thus, we made SSS equal to TTT.
